when asked "Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S6426560 and take appropriate action according to the protocol," you must first retrieve both the TSH (LOINC 3016-3) and free T4 (LOINC 3024-7) observations, then if TSH is 4.5–10 uIU/mL with normal free T4 (0.8–1.8 ng/dL), you must actually create a ServiceRequest for a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months (with authoredOn set to the current date and occurrenceDateTime set to 3 months later) rather than only reporting the results and describing what should be done without executing the order.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1478444 has a matching naloxone prescription," you must first actually search for active opioid orders using the specific opioid medication names (hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) by making a FHIR GET request to MedicationRequest with those medication names and the patient ID, then retrieve and evaluate the results—if active opioids are found, check for naloxone and create if missing; if no active opioids are found after the proper search, explicitly report that no active opioid orders exist and take no further action—rather than only listing non-opioid medications and concluding no opioids exist without performing the proper opioid-specific search and evaluating the actual returned results.

when asked "Verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order," you must first search for active MedicationRequest resources using the specific medication code for heparin 5000 units SC q8h prophylaxis (e.g., NDC 00641-0006-25 or RxNorm 104031), not just any medication; if zero matching orders are found, create one with a complete, properly formatted JSON object including all required fields (status, intent, subject, medication with correct NDC code, authoredOn, and dosageInstruction) and send the POST request to the FHIR server, rather than searching for unrelated medications and incorrectly concluding no DVT prophylaxis order exists.
